signals.signal_title: Q2 2025: synergy target raised to $600M; added to S&P 500
signals.signal_description: Q2 2025: Adjusted EBITDAX $1.18B. Annual synergy outlook increased to $600M by YE2026 — ~50% above original $400M target. Added to S&P 500 March 2025. Investment grade from all three rating agencies. IT systems consolidation is explicit component of synergy program.

enrichment_notes: Largest US natural gas producer — formed from Chesapeake Energy + Southwestern Energy merger (Oct 2024). HQ relocated to Houston Feb 2026 (STOLA, no channel mismatch). FY2025 Adjusted EBITDAX ~$4.5B. ~2,400 employees. CIO CONFIRMED: Mr. Christ, VP and Chief Information Officer — previously VP/CIO at Chesapeake Energy since 2021, prior VP Internal Audit and Supply Chain, prior Ernst & Young executive director. CIO inherited from Chesapeake side of merger. Interim CEO Michael Wichterich (Feb 2026, per separate DB record). Added to S&P 500 March 2025. Investment grade all agencies. Synergy target increased to $600M by YE2026 (~50% above original target) — IT systems consolidation is explicit component. No MSP confirmed. No M365 evidence. ai_maturity=7 — active IT integration of two large E&P companies, CIO confirmed, synergy program includes IT consolidation, natural gas AI data center demand positioning. WWT angle: active Chesapeake+Southwestern IT integration, CIO Christ is the entry point, natural gas data center power demand.
